text: As I enter this space, bathed in the glow of blue neon and sterile glass, I am struck by a sense of anticipation and slight alienation. The environment feels meticulously designed for observation and reflection—almost clinical in its orderliness. The "R+D" wall urges thoughts of progress and possibility, yet the artificiality of the lighting and the distant hum of technology creates a peculiar tension within me. I feel both curious and a bit uneasy, as if I am on the threshold of a new experiment, about to become both observer and subject in an unfolding scene of innovation and uncertainty.
